title: Erythroxylum obtusifolium
taxonomy: Erythroxylum obtusifolium is classified under the family Erythroxylaceae, commonly known as the coca family. This family consists of around 220 species, primarily found in the tropical regions of Central and South America. Erythroxylum obtusifolium is a member of the genus Erythroxylum, which includes several species of shrubs and trees. The classification of Erythroxylum obtusifolium also places it within the order Malpighiales, a large and diverse group of flowering plants. As a member of the Malpighiales order, Erythroxylum obtusifolium shares characteristics with other plants in this group, such as its tropical distribution and complex leaf morphology.

intro: Erythroxylum obtusifolium, also known as the South American coca shrub, is a plant species native to the Andean region, which has been used for centuries in traditional medicine. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, including pain relief, inflammation reduction, and improved cognitive function, although its psychoactive properties are minimal compared to Erythroxylum coca. The bioactive constituents of Erythroxylum obtusifolium include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make various herbal preparations, such as teas and infusions, which are used to treat a range of ailments, including arthritis, digestive issues, and fever.
